content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 27, 2000


Tyrone I. Vahedi
Law Offices of Tyrone I. Vahedi
11661 San Vicente Blvd., Suite 903
Los Angeles, CA 90049

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-00-202

Dear Mr. Vahedi:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1

QUESTION

Under the Act, what written disclosures are required for the slate mailer you intend to publish?

CONCLUSION

You must comply with the slate mailer identification and disclaimer requirements of section 84305.5, discussed below.

FACTS

You are requesting advice concerning the written disclosures required on a slate mailer that you wish to publish. You would like to call the slate mailer either: the "Official Democratic Vote by Mail/Poll Guide," the "Official Vote by Mail/Poll Guide," or the "Democratic Vote by Mail/Poll Guide."

_________________________

1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations. Because your letter presents a general question, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to regulation 18329(c). Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-00-202
Page No. 2

You plan to put the following statement in 14-point font just under the name of
the slate: "Featuring Concise Information about State Candidates & Issues Evaluations
and Recommendations by: Classroom Teachers, Police Officers, The Democratic Party,
Leading Democrats, Labor & Environmental Organizations, and others." You will also
list on the outside cover the disclosure required by section 84305.5 in the appropriate font
size.

You ask whether there are any other disclosures that you are required to list, or
any other disclosures required based on each of the three possible names you are
considering above.

ANALYSIS

State Mailer Identification and Disclaimer Requirements.

The Act requires candidates and committees that send over 200 substantially
similar pieces of mail to include the name and address of the sender on each mailing.
(Sections 82041.5, 84305.) Section 84305.5 imposes additional identification and
disclaimer requirements on slate mailer organizations that send slate mailers. A copy of
section 84305.5, detailing these requirements, is enclosed. A "slate mailer" means over
200 substantially similar pieces of mail that support or oppose four or more candidates or
ballot measures. (Sections 82041.5, 82048.3.) A "slate mailer organization" is an
organization that receives $500 or more per calendar year to produce slate mailers.
(Section 82048.4.) Slate mailers must include the following written disclosures:

• Name and address. A slate mailer must include the name and address of the
slate mailer organization that produced the mailer in the font and location stated in
section 84305.5(a)(1).

• Disclaimer. The slate mailer must include the "Notice to Voters" text in a box
and in the typeface prescribed by section 84305.5(a)(2) (see enclosed statute). The notice
to voters states that the slate mailer was not sent by an official political party
organization, and notes that candidates or ballot measures that have paid to be in the slate
mailer are designated by an asterisk.

• Designation of candidates and measures that have paid to be in the slate
mailer. Under section 84305.5(a)(4), the slate mailer must designate by an asterisk each
candidate and ballot measure that has paid to appear in the slate mailer.

• Endorsement of Opposite Party Candidates. Section 84305.5(a)(5)
provides:

"(a) No slate mailer organization or committee primarily formed to
support or oppose one or more ballot measures shall send a slate mailer
unless:

* * *

File No. I-00-202
Page No. 3

(5) The name of any candidate appearing in the slate mailer who is a
member of a political party differing from the political party which the
mailer appears by representation or indicia to represent is accompanied,
immediately below the name, by the party designation of the candidate, in
no less than 9-point roman type which shall be in a color or print that
contrasts with the background so as to be easily legible. The designation
shall not be required in the case of candidates for nonpartisan office."

According to the legislative history of this provision, section 84305.5(a)(5) is
designed to prevent slate mailer organizations from mixing parties on party-designed
slates that feature candidates who are members of the opposite party. (Senate Committee
on Elections and Reapportionment, Analysis of Senate Bill 313, March 6, 1991.)

Section 84305.5(a)(5) applies to mailers that appear to expressly represent a
political party (i.e., "Your Democratic Ballot," "Republican Voter Guide," or the
inclusion of a symbol representing a political party). Section 84305.5(a)(5) also applies
to mailers that appear by indicia to represent a political party. The word "indicia" refers
to "postal markings often imprinted on mail." (Meriam-Webster's Collegiate Dict. (10th
ed. 1996) p. 592.) Under section 84305.5(a)(1), a slate mailer organization must place
the name of the organization on its slate mailers. As such, the name of the organization
on the slate mailer becomes a postal marking. Thus, the language of section
84305.5(a)(5) contemplates that a slate mailer can appear to represent a political party
by the name of the slate mailer organization printed on the mailer.

Two of the names you have proposed using for the slate mailer, the "Official
Democratic Vote by Mail/Poll Guide" and the "Democratic Vote by Mail/Poll Guide"
make the slate mailer appear to represent the view of the Democratic Party. If you
choose either of these names, and plan to endorse any Republican candidates on your
slate mailer, under section 84305.5(a)(5), the mailer must include the party designation of
any Republican candidate endorsed in the mailer.

• Endorsement of candidate that differs from official party endorsement. In
addition, section 84305.5(a)(6) requires that if you endorse a candidate in the slate mailer
that differs from the official endorsement of the political party which the mailer appears
to represent, immediately below the endorsement you must state: THIS IS NOT THE
POSITION OF THE (political party which the mailer appears to represent) PARTY.

I hope this answers your questions about slate mailer identification requirements.
I have also enclosed a handbook on campaign disclosure for slate mailer organizations.

File No. 1-00-202
Page No. 4

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures
